name: fractal-docs-generator
description: 目录级 CLAUDE.md 生成。触发：mkdir、create directory、目录结构变更。
capabilities: Generates CLAUDE.md files when directories are created or modified
Creates documentation tables listing files with their role and responsibility
Uses standard role categories: entry point, core, utility, types, config, tests
Works alongside file-header-guardian for complete project documentation
author: Dimon94
securitySummary: All 11 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted Chinese metadata text as cryptographic algorithms and markdown template syntax as shell execution patterns. This is a documentation generator skill that creates CLAUDE.md files. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Projects often lack documentation for directory structures. This skill automatically generates CLAUDE.md files with file responsibility tables when directories are created or modified, keeping documentation synchronized with code architecture.
limitations: Does not analyze file contents to determine responsibilities automatically
Requires manual specification of file roles and responsibilities
Only generates markdown tables, does not write prose documentation
Triggered by directory operations, not file content changes

useCases.targetUser: Feature developers working on modular codebases
useCases.title: New feature module documentation
useCases.description: When creating a new feature directory, automatically generate a CLAUDE.md documenting the module structure and file responsibilities.

useCases.title: Team onboarding documentation
useCases.description: Maintain up-to-date project structure documentation that helps new team members understand the codebase organization.

useCases.title: Architecture consistency enforcement
useCases.description: Ensure all project directories follow consistent documentation patterns with standardized file role tables.

promptTemplates.title: Basic directory documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a CLAUDE.md for the new {directory_name} directory. Include a table listing the main files with their role and one-sentence responsibility.
promptTemplates.scenario: When creating a new feature or module directory

promptTemplates.title: Complete module documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive CLAUDE.md for the {directory_name} module. Include all files with their role (entry, core, utility, types, config, test) and specific responsibility description.
promptTemplates.scenario: When setting up a new microservice or major component

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring documentation update
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the CLAUDE.md in {directory_name} to reflect the new file structure after the refactor. Add new files and remove deprecated ones.
promptTemplates.scenario: After restructuring a module or component

promptTemplates.title: Cross-module documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create CLAUDE.md files for multiple related directories: {directories}. Ensure consistent role classifications across all modules.
promptTemplates.scenario: When establishing documentation standards across multiple modules

bestPractices: Use consistent role categories across all directories for predictable documentation structure
Keep responsibility descriptions brief and focused on the file's external interface
Update CLAUDE.md immediately after adding or removing files in the directory
Coordinate with file-header-guardian for complete project documentation coverage
antiPatterns: Leaving responsibility descriptions empty or too vague
Inconsistent role classifications across different directories
Creating CLAUDE.md without reviewing the documented file responsibilities
Forgetting to update documentation when refactoring moves files between directories

faq.question: What triggers this skill?
faq.answer: The skill triggers on directory creation, renaming, moving, or when files within a directory change. It does not trigger on file content changes alone.

faq.question: How are file roles determined?
faq.answer: File roles are specified manually by the developer. The skill provides standard categories but does not analyze file contents.

faq.question: Can I customize the template format?
faq.answer: The skill uses a standard markdown table format. You can edit the generated CLAUDE.md to add additional sections as needed.

faq.question: Does this work with nested directories?
faq.answer: Yes, each directory can have its own CLAUDE.md. The skill triggers on any directory structure change at any depth.

faq.question: How does this differ from file-header-guardian?
faq.answer: fractal-docs-generator manages directory-level CLAUDE.md files, while file-header-guardian manages file-level documentation headers. They work together for complete coverage.

faq.question: What file roles are supported?
faq.answer: Standard roles include: entry point, core logic, utility, type definitions, configuration, and test files. You can add custom roles as needed.
